creator: netchild@FreeBSD.org
text:
Quoting Stefan Walter <stefan@freebsd.org> (from Sun, 4 Jan 2009 =20
14:02:14 +0100):

> Hi Simon, hi Alexander,
>
> please see the message quoted below. Any news on the icc update [1]?

I don't have the time to have a look at porting it (not much spare =20
time). In case I get some time, my priorities are somewhere else. I =20
should be able to review (but not test) patches. I can also answer =20
questions.

Regarding the comment in the PR: I am not aware of a native icc =20
version for FreeBSD. Until icc 8.x I was the main contact regarding =20
icc on the FreeBSD side. My main contact at Intels side was not =20
directly in the compiler group, he was in the embedded systems group =20
back then, but the compiler group was helpful via the official icc bug =20
report channel. If there's some native icc version in the works for =20
FreeBSD, then it is not me who knows about it. In case someone is =20
aware of some ongoing work in this regard, I could provide some =20
background infos which may or may not speed up the work.

Bye,
Alexander.

> Regards,
> Stefan
>
> [1]: http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3Dports/108795
>
> ----- Forwarded message from Eric De La Cruz Lugo =20
> <eric_delacruz@yahoo.com> -----
>
> From: Eric De La Cruz Lugo <eric_delacruz@yahoo.com>
> Subject: icc  Current Status
> To: stefan@freebsd.org
> Cc: freebsd-ports-bugs@freebsd.org
> Date: Thu, 1 Jan 2009 19:39:32 -0800 (PST)
>
>> Hi, Stefan:
>> I
>> was wondering about the Status for the intel Compiler "icc V. 10 " ,
>> there are any news about it?
>> I am into a cluster (HPC) project for this Year (2009), and am trying to
>> install FreeBSD 7 or 6.4 at least, but one requierement for the Physics
>> and Math Guys is the Intel Compiler.
>>
>> Thanks in advance on this.
>> Eric De La Cruz Lugo
>> Merida, Yucatan, Mexico.
>
> ----- End forwarded message -----
>
>
